text.title: Prompt Changelog Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that defeat the discipline in practice. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Silent edit — engineer edits a prompt file without a changelog entry; pre-commit hook missing or bypassed.
failure-mode.detector: git history shows a commit that changed a prompts/*.txt OR prompts.yaml file without a corresponding entry in PROMPTS.changelog.md
failure-mode.repair: Install the pre-commit hook globally. CI also runs the check; PRs that lack an entry are auto-blocked at merge. Backfill missing entries with the best available intent reconstruction and an `eval_delta_unknown` flag so future readers know the data is partial.

failure-mode.description: Eval skipped — engineer claims an improvement, eval delta is hand-typed instead of CI-runner-computed.
failure-mode.detector: eval_delta values appear in the entry but no matching eval-runner artifact exists in CI for the entry's timestamp
failure-mode.repair: Require eval-runner attestation: the runner posts a signed JSON artifact that the pre-commit hook checks. Hand-typed deltas are rejected. If the eval cannot run for the prompt change (no suitable suite), the eval_delta field carries `unevaluable` with a one-line reason.

failure-mode.description: Inline prompts persist — codebase still has triple-quoted prompts in source files, defeating hashability.
failure-mode.detector: grep returns multiline string literals > 30 lines in source files that call LLM SDKs
failure-mode.repair: Refactor sprint: extract every inline prompt to prompts/*.txt with a load_prompt() helper. Pre-commit lint blocks new inline prompts from this point forward.

failure-mode.description: Rollback hash missing — entry omits the rollback target, MTTR spikes when a regression is detected because the team has to find the previous hash manually.
failure-mode.detector: entry.rollback_hash empty OR not equal to entry.prev_hash for any entry past the first
failure-mode.repair: Pre-commit hook auto-fills rollback_hash from prev_hash unless explicitly overridden (e.g. rolling back further to skip a known-bad intermediate). Reviewer confirms the rollback target before merge.

failure-mode.description: Deploy summary missing — release includes prompt changes, but no deploy-summary.md exists; on-call has no quick map from "regression in production" to "which prompt edit caused it".
failure-mode.detector: release branch tag exists AND deploy-summary.md was not committed in the release PR
failure-mode.repair: Release gate blocks deploys without a deploy-summary.md when any prompt changed since the last release. The summary auto-generates from changelog entries in the release window; engineer reviews and signs off.

failure-mode.description: Changelog clutter — entries accumulate for trivial edits (whitespace, single-word changes that the eval cannot detect), drowning the signal.
failure-mode.detector: median entry has eval_delta = unevaluable OR intent string is shorter than 20 chars
failure-mode.repair: Define a minimum-meaningful-change threshold: edits that do not change the normalised hash (pure whitespace, comment-only) skip the changelog. Quarterly cleanup squashes trivial entries.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: A prompt edit accompanied by an LLM-generated intent string ("improved prompt clarity for better outputs") is a smell — these are template fillers from coding assistants and should be replaced by the engineer's actual hypothesis. Reviewer rejects template-shaped intents.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Monthly: ML lead reviews the changelog of the past 30 days, identifies one regression pattern (e.g. "edits intended to reduce false positives consistently degrade recall"), and updates the team's eval suite to catch that pattern before deploy.
